issue_title: Overwrite button disabled in paste conflict dialog

reasoning_trace.finding: PowerShell search found paste-conflict strings in app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml and the main implementation in app/src/main/java/com/amaze/filemanager/asynchronous/asynctasks/movecopy/PreparePasteTask.kt.
Confirmed relevant UI strings: paste, skip, overwrite, rename, doforall, checking_conflicts, same_dir_overwrite_error.

PreparePasteTask checks destination children for same-name conflicts, builds a MaterialDialog with Rename as positive, Skip as neutral, and Overwrite as negative, then waits for the selected DialogAction.

The custom dialog layout contains only the conflict text, file name, and 'Do for all items' checkbox. The Skip/Overwrite/Rename buttons are MaterialDialog action buttons configured in PreparePasteTask.kt.
prepareDialog sets dialogBuilder.negativeText(R.string.overwrite), so DialogAction.NEGATIVE is the Overwrite button.
The paste snackbar gets the current MainFragment path and passes it as targetPath to new PreparePasteTask(mainActivity).execute(...), with the copied files as source HybridFileParcelable objects.
For the reproduction, copying File1.mp3 and pressing Paste while still in the same folder means targetPath equals the copied file's parent directory.
Drag-and-drop also calls PreparePasteTask.execute with a pasteLocation and file list, confirming PreparePasteTask is the shared concrete paste-conflict entry point.

reasoning_trace.target: Overwrite disabled because the source file parent equals targetPath
reasoning_trace.finding: In showDialog, if hybridFileParcelable.getParent(context.get()) == targetPath, the code disables DialogAction.NEGATIVE. Since negative is Overwrite, same-directory paste conflicts always show Overwrite disabled.
MoveFiles comments direct callers to use PreparePasteTask; it is not responsible for rendering the paste conflict dialog.
CopyService receives TAG_COPY_SOURCES and TAG_COPY_TARGET and copies each source into targetPath/name. It has no UI disable logic.
GenericCopyUtil performs the actual byte copy into the target file. It is downstream of the dialog decision and does not control whether Overwrite is selectable.
FileUtils read was used to inspect related file utility behavior; no paste conflict dialog or Overwrite button enablement exists there.

CopyNode has explicit RENAME and SKIP branches but no OVERWRITE branch; this is intentional because leaving the file in filesToCopy with the original name causes CopyService to write to the conflicting target name.
The checkbox only sets renameAll, overwriteAll, or skipAll after a button is clicked. It cannot enable a disabled Overwrite button, matching the report that checking/unchecking it does not help.
This statement directly grays out the Overwrite button for the reproduction path and no neighboring code re-enables it.

fault_candidates.method: showDialog(MutableList<HybridFileParcelable> conflictingFiles, HashMap<HybridFileParcelable, String> conflictingDirActionMap, CopyDialogBinding copyDialogBinding, MaterialDialog.Builder dialogBuilder, AppCompatCheckBox checkBox)
fault_candidates.reasoning: The dialog labels DialogAction.NEGATIVE as Overwrite, then disables that action whenever the copied file's parent equals the paste target. In the reported reproduction, copying a file and pasting in the same directory makes that condition true, so Overwrite is shown permanently disabled and cannot complete DialogAction.NEGATIVE.

final_answer.method: showDialog(MutableList<HybridFileParcelable> conflictingFiles, HashMap<HybridFileParcelable, String> conflictingDirActionMap, CopyDialogBinding copyDialogBinding, MaterialDialog.Builder dialogBuilder, AppCompatCheckBox checkBox)
final_answer.causal_chain: PasteHelper passes the current directory as targetPath to PreparePasteTask.execute. checkConflicts finds a same-name file in that target and calls prepareDialog. prepareDialog assigns Overwrite to DialogAction.NEGATIVE. showDialog then sees hybridFileParcelable.getParent(context.get()) == targetPath for the same-directory paste case and executes .isEnabled = false on DialogAction.NEGATIVE. Since that action is Overwrite and no later code re-enables it, the dialog displays Overwrite grayed out while Skip and Rename remain usable.
final_answer.fix_hint: Remove or narrow the same-parent disable logic for DialogAction.NEGATIVE so the Overwrite button remains enabled when a paste conflict is presented.
